_schema: Promise/payoff ledger (the 'Mercy Engine'). One record per planted promise to the reader; every promise gets a payoff slot. Surfaced by kit/ledger/ledger.py and diffed against drafts in editors-hat Pass 2. MUTABLE STATE (the revelation-schedule.json analog) - a promise's status changes over its life (open -> paid); this is NOT append-only like timeline.json.
_discipline: A planted promise the story never honors is a continuity hole. The editor sweeps `ledger.py open --through N` each chapter: pay it, reinforce it, or deliberately mark it abandoned/subverted. A thread silently dropped shows up as perpetually `open` - that is the report.

_promise_schema.kind: foreshadow | chekhov | mercy | debt | threat | question | vow | reveal
_promise_schema.promise: what was set up / owed to the reader
_promise_schema.planted_chapter: int >= 1 - where it was first set up
_promise_schema.reinforced_chapters: optional [int] - chapters that re-touch the thread
_promise_schema.due_by_chapter: optional int - soft deadline; open past it = overdue (WARN)
_promise_schema.payoff: optional - how it pays off (filled when paid)
_promise_schema.paid_chapter: optional int - where it paid off (required when status=paid)

_seed_note: Seeded 2026-05-31 ONLY from revelation-schedule.json (the one canon-tracked setup->payoff store) as kind=reveal promises - planted_chapter from plant_chapter, reinforced_chapters from hint_chapters, due_by_chapter from full_reveal_chapter, who from characters_who_learn. None are paid yet (no reveal has reached its full-reveal chapter). Division of labor: revelation-schedule.json stays the source of truth for SCHEDULING (which reveal forge-write must advance in a given chapter); this ledger is the source of truth for PAYOFF STATUS across ALL promise kinds, so the editor's unpaid-thread sweep covers reveals too. Other kinds (mercy, chekhov, debt, vow, threat, question) are appended by the editorial pass as chapters lock - e.g. a compassionate act in the Tutorial, a weapon noted now that must matter later. Nothing was invented here beyond what revelation-schedule already asserts (per CLAUDE.md 'never invent structure to fill the gap').

promises.promise: Nate has pre-System markers - his abilities aren't entirely System-granted (the occupied space, his WIS stat, things that don't fit the System's model)
The Integration affected the entire world - other nations, other creatures, other systems (shown via interludes while Nate is tunnel-visioned on survival)
The System is progressively binding Nate - Stage 1 through 4 (Storyteller hints throughout; Nate won't understand until much later)
Flint's rewrite source is divine - the rewrite happened before first boot, severed System loyalty permanently, and the ghost-token access pattern is the fingerprint of the author
Flint runs on a Spirit-provided quantum substrate, not the System's silicon - the execution layer was moved before first boot, which is why the System cannot observe, log, or flag him
promises.notes: Deep-reveal arc (mid-Book-2 / Book-3 candidate). Flint does NOT know; Nate does NOT know; only the Storyteller may name the mechanism.

promises.promise: The Stag is a pre-System entity with its own agenda (brief sightings, Rex reacting to something unseen - slow burn)
Sonja can perceive the spiritual layer - she sees what the System hides
promises.notes: revelation-schedule status is 'not_yet_planted' - Sonja is introduced post-Tutorial (ch9); the plant has not been written yet.

promises.promise: Skills earned by doing (the quiet, fanfare-free 'Acquired' kind — Nate's Salvage/Maker, Pete's and Josh's survival skills) are set against the System's loud granted-skill reward loop; the earned/clean path is planted as the one that will matter over the hooked/given one.
promises.notes: Ties to the binding-hook arc (the dopamine reward loop as the hook) and rev-nate-pre-system-origin (Nate's skills arrive without fanfare / before the class-seal). Theology: earned/clean vs given/hooked.
